description: Machine-readable declaration for the six full-engine API parity claims rendered in README.md and docs/GTS-API-CLI-PARITY.md.

api_shape.contract: Parse a byte buffer or path as a CBOR Sequence, verify the id/prev chain, fold every recoverable frame, and return a graph/result with diagnostics instead of panicking on malformed input.
api_shape.current_native_surface: Python `gts.read(data, keys=None, expected_head=None, allow_segments=True)`; Rust `reader::read(&bytes, allow_segments, expected_head)` or `reader::read_with_options` with `ReadOptions::with_content_key`; Go `reader.Read(data, allowSegments, expectedHead)`; TypeScript `Read(bytes, allowSegments, expectedHead?)`; Smalltalk `GtsReader read:allowSegments:`; Kotlin `read(data, allowSegments)`.

api_shape.contract: Apply strict transport checks over the same fold: chain/hash diagnostics, expected-head freshness when provided, streamable-layout checks when requested, and COSE signature status when keys are provided.
api_shape.current_native_surface: CLI `gts verify`; Python `gts.verify.verify_file`; Rust `gmeow_gts::verify::verify_file` plus folded diagnostics and lower-level COSE helpers in every engine.

api_shape.contract: Emit deterministic CBOR for hashed or signed bytes, compute each frame id from its content, and set `prev` to the previous frame id.
api_shape.current_native_surface: Python `Writer`; Rust `writer::Writer`; Go `writer.New`; TypeScript `Writer`; Smalltalk `GtsWriter`; Kotlin `Writer`.

api_shape.contract: Return the deterministic GTS value fold: terms, quads, reifiers, annotations, blobs, suppressions, opaque nodes, signatures, segment heads, profiles, and streamable layout state.
Project the folded RDF dataset to sorted N-Quads text with the same value semantics across engines.
api_shape.current_native_surface: Python `to_nquads`; Rust `nquads::to_nquads`; Go `nquads.ToNQuads`; TypeScript `toNQuads`; Smalltalk `GtsNQuads`; Kotlin `toNQuads`.

api_shape.contract: Build a GTS file from N-Quads text using the shared writer semantics.
api_shape.current_native_surface: Python `from_nquads`; Rust `from_nquads::from_nquads`; Go `fromnquads.FromNQuads`; TypeScript `fromNQuads`; Smalltalk `GtsFromNQuads`; Kotlin `fromNQuads`; CLI `gts from-nq` in every engine.

api_shape.contract: Project a default-graph RDF dataset to N-Triples and rebuild GTS bytes from N-Triples text using the shared RDF 1.2 parser/serializer.
api_shape.current_native_surface: Rust `rdf_codecs::to_ntriples` / `from_ntriples` behind `--features rdf-codecs`; Go `rdfcodecs.ToNTriples` / `FromNTriples`; CLI `gts to-nt` and `gts from-nt` in Rust and Go.

api_shape.contract: Project a default-graph RDF dataset to RDF/XML and rebuild GTS bytes from RDF/XML text, including RDF/XML namespace, parseType, collection, reification, annotation, and RDF 1.2 triple-term grammar.
api_shape.current_native_surface: Rust `rdf_codecs::to_rdf_xml` / `from_rdf_xml` behind `--features rdf-codecs`; Go `rdfcodecs.ToRDFXML` / `FromRDFXML`; CLI `gts to-rdfxml` and `gts from-rdfxml` in Rust and Go.

api_shape.contract: Project folded RDF to readable TriG graph blocks and rebuild GTS bytes from the supported TriG surface without changing N-Quads content.
api_shape.current_native_surface: Python `gts.trig.to_trig` / `from_trig`; Rust `trig::to_trig` / `from_trig::from_trig`; Rust `rdf_codecs::to_trig` / `from_trig` with `--features rdf-codecs`; Go `rdfcodecs.ToTriG` / `FromTriG`; CLI `gts to-trig` and `gts from-trig` in Python, Rust, and Go.

api_shape.contract: Project a default-graph RDF dataset to Turtle and rebuild GTS bytes from Turtle text using the shared Turtle-family RDF 1.2 parser/serializer.
api_shape.current_native_surface: Rust `rdf_codecs::to_turtle` / `from_turtle` behind `--features rdf-codecs`; Go `rdfcodecs.ToTurtle` / `FromTurtle`; CLI `gts to-turtle` and `gts from-turtle` in Rust and Go.

api_shape.contract: Expose resolved access to terms, quads, reifier bindings, annotations, suppressions, blobs, opaque nodes, signatures, diagnostics, segment heads, profiles, metadata, and streamable state.
api_shape.current_native_surface: Native fields on `Graph`/`GtsGraph` in all six engines, with helper lookups where idiomatic.

api_shape.contract: Preserve inline blob bytes by `blake3:<hex>` digest and retain declared blob metadata such as media type. Extraction MUST re-hash bytes before writing them. Implementations MAY keep transformed blob bytes lazy until access.
api_shape.current_native_surface: Python `Graph.blobs`/`blob_meta`; Rust `Graph.blobs` lazy `BlobEntry` plus `blob_entry`/`blob_bytes`/`decoded_blobs`; Go `Graph.Blobs`/`BlobMeta`; TypeScript `Graph.blobs`/`blobMeta`; Smalltalk `GtsGraph blobs`/`blobMeta`; Kotlin `Graph.blobs`/`blobMeta`.

api_shape.contract: Preserve undecodable or unsupported recoverable frames as graph-visible opaque nodes with a frame id, frame type, reason, and signature status.
Preserve stable diagnostic `code` values and optional frame indexes; native detail text may differ.

Carry read mode, segment allowance, expected head, key provider, recursion/decode budgets, and streamable validation as options. Engines MAY stage these as separate helpers while preserving the same observable fold and diagnostics.
api_shape.current_native_surface: Python `keys`, Rust `ReadOptions`/`read_to_sink_with_options`/`read_to_sink_from_reader`, Go `reader.Options`/`reader.ReadToSink`, TypeScript `allowSegments`/`foldStreamToSink`, Smalltalk `allowSegments`, Kotlin `allowSegments`, and CLI flags today; deeper recursion/MMR options are future Full Reader work.

evidence.text: fun read(data: ByteArray, allowSegments: Boolean = true, expectedHead: ByteArray? = null): Graph

TypeScript.deferred_reason: TriG transform parity is intentionally deferred until it can share the Python/Rust round-trip expectations.

Smalltalk.deferred_reason: TriG transform parity is intentionally deferred until it can share the Python/Rust round-trip expectations.

Kotlin.deferred_reason: TriG transform parity is intentionally deferred until it can share the Python/Rust round-trip expectations.

cells.Rust: `rdf` feature (native dataset model); `native-store` feature (native in-memory store)

Go.deferred_reason: Native RDF/store adapters are intentionally deferred for Go until an ecosystem adapter lands.

TypeScript.deferred_reason: Native RDF/store adapters are intentionally deferred for TypeScript until an ecosystem adapter lands.

Smalltalk.deferred_reason: Native RDF/store adapters are intentionally deferred for Smalltalk/Pharo until an ecosystem adapter lands.

Kotlin.deferred_reason: Native RDF/store adapters are intentionally deferred for Kotlin/JVM until an ecosystem adapter lands.

Go.deferred_reason: Relational exports are intentionally deferred for Go.

TypeScript.deferred_reason: Relational exports are intentionally deferred for TypeScript.

Smalltalk.deferred_reason: Relational exports are intentionally deferred for Smalltalk/Pharo.

Kotlin.deferred_reason: Relational exports are intentionally deferred for Kotlin/JVM.
